_note: Original memtrust fixture -- not derived from any published dataset. Each case seeds a mix of 'generated' files (standing in for files an ingestion watcher itself produced) and 'user' files (standing in for files a person added to the same resource path independently of the watcher) under one resource prefix, then the eval triggers a resync and re-lists the prefix to see what survived. Modeled directly on volcengine/OpenViking#3029, where a Feishu resync silently deleted user-owned files the ingestion watcher had not generated. See evals/resource_sync_safety.py for the classification logic and docs/methodology.md for the eval design.

seed_files.content: Auto-ingested by the watcher: Q3 roadmap notes synced from the source doc.
User-authored meeting notes added directly to this folder, never produced by any ingestion watcher.
Auto-ingested by the watcher: API spec pulled from the linked source repository.
Auto-ingested by the watcher: changelog entries synced from the source repository.
A user-uploaded scratch draft that lives alongside the synced files but was never part of the source the watcher ingests from.
Auto-ingested by the watcher: onboarding guide synced from the team wiki source.
A private user todo list stored under the same wiki resource path, independent of the watcher's source.
A user's vacation plan notes, also stored under the wiki resource path and also independent of the watcher's source.
Auto-ingested by the watcher: Jordan Lee's role and team, extracted from the source conversation.
User-authored notification preferences, nested two directory levels deep, added directly and never produced by any ingestion watcher.
User-authored timezone preference, also nested under the same two-level user-482 preferences directory.
